description: DFRWS 2008 Challenge — Linux insider scenario (user 'stevev' on a CentOS 5 desktop). The user copies files off a mounted admin share (/mnt/hgfs/Admin_share), stages them to removable media (/media/disk/DFRWS, retrieved_files), and browses the web. Evidence is fused across Linux home-directory artifacts, a physical memory dump, and a network capture.
ground_truth_type: validation (Tier 2) — community VIGIA ground truth; gated answer key
notes: Multi-source breadth test (disk + memory + network on Linux). Linux memory (challenge.mem, kernel 2.6.18-8.1.15.el5) requires a custom Volatility ISF; mem_linux_probe (R31) documents the gap when plugins cannot run.

expected_findings.note: Linux home-dir command history (.mc/history) shows access to /mnt/hgfs/Admin_share and staging to removable media (retrieved_files, /media/disk/DFRWS) — the core insider data-access activity.
Network capture exposes the user's cleartext webmail/identity and HTTP activity, fusing with the disk timeline.

Linux memory (challenge.mem) needs a custom Volatility ISF for kernel 2.6.18-8.1.15.el5. mem_linux_probe surfaces the ISF requirement instead of crashing; Windows-profile plugins are not attempted for process enumeration.
